name: Effigy: Credit brokerage platform
description: Public marketing site and credit simulators for a Portuguese credit intermediary, with a layered PHP JSON API, hardened admin surface, and a React SPA—architecture built around validation, atomic persistence, and explicit security middleware.
longDescription: End-to-end delivery for Effigy (intermediação de crédito): a Vite + React 18 SPA for product pages, config-driven simulators (housing, personal, car, credit transfer, works), team and trusted-partners sections fed from a unified REST API under /api/, and contact / simulation flows persisted via a PHP 8 backend with file-based JSON storage and audited logging. The backend is organised as an explicit middleware chain (CORS, CSRF, auth, HTTPS enforcement for admin) so cross-cutting security and transport policy stay centralised rather than scattered through handlers. Admin routes protect CMS operations for taxas (rates, ECB/BdP/AT integrations), equipa, parceiros, and brand/social assets. Submissions use per-IP JSON files, exclusive locks on the shared contacts registry, and atomic writes (temp file + rename). The public simulator path debounces auto-submit, uses a 15s client timeout, retries transient 5xx/network failures, and deduplicates identical payloads within 60s so “saved” only reflects a successful API response. Shared constants in shared/ keep the SPA and PHP contracts aligned at build time. The stack targets cPanel and Docker (PHP-FPM + Nginx).
features: Layered PHP middleware for transport policy, authentication, and CSRF—keeps handlers focused on domain logic
Config-driven credit simulators with live rates from /api/taxas
Partners and team sections with image assets from /api/assets
Contact and simulator submissions with server-side validation; simulator client: debounced submit, 15s timeout, retries on 5xx/network, 60s deduplication
Submission storage: per-IP JSON under data/submissions-by-ip, flock on contacts file, writeJsonFile atomic overwrite (tmp + rename)
Admin CMS: rates, team, partners, brand/icons/social images
ECB / BdP / AT rate fetch workflows and eligibility metadata
Cookie banner, legal pages, and WhatsApp / modals aligned with marketing UX
cPanel-oriented PHP build with data/.htaccess denying direct file access
highlights: Single /api surface for public and admin consumers with a consistent security envelope
Concurrency-safe persistence: flock, atomic writes, and data/ kept off direct HTTP
Session + CSRF model for all mutating admin operations; secrets and signing keys only from environment
challenges: Financial marketing sites need accurate simulator behaviour, editable rates without redeploys, and strict protection of admin and submission data on shared hosting.
solutions: Implemented a thin PHP router with explicit middleware (CORS, CSRF, auth, HTTPS for admin), JSON registries for taxas and assets, and a React admin that mirrors the same contracts as the public app. Submissions use locked read–modify–write on the contacts index and per-IP files, atomic full-file writes, and data/ kept out of direct HTTP reach. The simulator layer aligns UX with reliability: debounced POSTs, bounded wait, controlled retries, and duplicate suppression without claiming success until the API responds OK.
security: Administrator authentication: server-side credential from environment configuration, verified with SHA-256 using a constant-time comparison; session cookie with HttpOnly, Secure (production), and SameSite; idle timeout and session invalidation on application restart
Cross-site request forgery (CSRF) protection: synchroniser token required on state-changing requests, validated from a custom HTTP request header
Rate limiting on login; configurable CORS allowlist
HTTPS enforcement middleware for admin in production
Upload validation: allowed MIME categories, max size, whitelist paths for asset serving (path traversal checks)
Session signing key and other secrets supplied only via environment configuration, excluded from version control
Exclusive file locks (flock) on contacts / per-IP JSON; Apache .htaccess blocks direct HTTP access to data/
